preconditions: x and y must be lists of numbers
x and y must have the same length
x and y must not be empty
postconditions: return value is the covariance between x and y
edge_cases: x and y have only one element
x and y have identical elements
x and y have elements with extreme values

preconditions: x and y must be lists of strings
x and y must have the same length
x and y must not be empty
postconditions: returns a float representing the covariance of x and y
the result is a valid covariance value
edge_cases: if x or y is empty, raises ValueError
if x and y have different lengths, raises ValueError

agreed.groq: x and y must have the same length
agreed.hf: x and y must have the same length

agreed.groq: x and y must not be empty
agreed.hf: x and y must not be empty

groq_only: x and y must be lists of numbers
hf_only: x and y must be lists of strings

groq_only: return value is the covariance between x and y
hf_only: returns a float representing the covariance of x and y
the result is a valid covariance value

groq_only: x and y have only one element
x and y have identical elements
x and y have elements with extreme values
hf_only: if x or y is empty, raises ValueError
if x and y have different lengths, raises ValueError
